acceptance_targets: [chapter_18_f01] In the revised span beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165 and still active through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203, the Eli/Bernadette parallel is inferable from Lena's edits, Bernadette's quoted language, and June's text without more than one abstract explanatory sentence after the restored quote at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:181. The passage must show at least one concrete editorial keep/cut decision with a stated client or money consequence.

[chapter_18_f03] In the revised ending beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223 and resolving by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:233, Lena's choice of the human room is clear through action or exchange alone, with no direct equivalent of the explanatory statements currently at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:231.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised span beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165 and still active through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203, the Eli/Bernadette parallel is inferable from Lena's edits, Bernadette's quoted language, and June's text without more than one abstract explanatory sentence after the restored quote at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:181. The passage must show at least one concrete editorial keep/cut decision with a stated client or money consequence.
findings.problem: The Bernadette transcription scene begins as strong embodied work, but then over-explains its own analogy to Eli. After the restored quote lands, the narration shifts into essay mode about smoothing voices and classed acceptability, which dilutes scene pressure and makes the chapter state its theme instead of earning it through Lena's live editorial choices.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:175, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203 so the realization comes through concrete transcription decisions under client pressure. Keep Bernadette's vivid material presence and June's interruption, but compress the abstract explanation after the restored line and replace it with one or two specific keep/cut choices that show Lena refusing to sand Bernadette into a grant-friendly brochure version of herself.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised ending beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223 and resolving by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:233, Lena's choice of the human room is clear through action or exchange alone, with no direct equivalent of the explanatory statements currently at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:231.
findings.problem: The closing image has the right human texture, but the narration then explains the chapter's thesis instead of trusting the room. 'Nobody had been built to catch her at the precise angle of her loneliness' and 'They were what was here' translate an already legible ending into statement, which costs the last beat some of its force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the ending span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:231 so Lena's turn toward ordinary, flawed human company is shown through an observable action inside the soup-room scene. Keep the coriander, radio static, argument, and overlapping voices, but replace the thesis sentences with a bodily or social choice: answering someone, taking a bowl, joining the joke, or otherwise entering the room in a way the reader can witness.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
